decks.notes: This deck revolves around using powerful artifacts like Coveted Jewel and The One Ring to generate massive amounts of mana and card advantage. It utilizes interactive spells like Force of Will and Sink into Stupor to disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up its own combo. By efficiently tutoring for key pieces with Transmute Artifact and Stock Up, the deck aims to assemble a game-winning board state quickly. The inclusion of powerful artifacts like Paradox Engine and Karn, the Great Creator further solidifies the deck's ability to outpace and overpower opponents in the Vintage format.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on card advantage and disruption, utilizing powerful draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Brainstorm. The deck aims to control the game through counterspells such as Force of Will and Flusterstorm, while also using Tinker and Yawgmoth's Will to help set up win conditions like Time Vault. The presence of powerful mana accelerants like Black Lotus and the Moxen ensure the deck can quickly develop a strong board presence. Overall, the strategy revolves around outsmarting and outlasting opponents through a combination of control, disruption, and powerful win conditions.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifact creatures and powerful artifacts to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. By combining creatures like Glaring Fleshraker and Scrawling Crawler with powerful artifacts such as The One Ring and Mystic Forge, the deck aims to create an unstoppable board presence and quickly close out the game. With additional support from cards like Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Karn, the Great Creator, the deck can easily control the battlefield and secure victory through efficient and aggressive plays.

This Vintage 1 vs 1 Combo - Paradoxical Outcome deck focuses on using card draw and mana acceleration to set up powerful combo plays. The deck utilizes cards like Thundertrap Trainer and Paradoxical Outcome to generate massive card advantage and mana advantage, enabling explosive turns where the player can draw through their deck and cast multiple spells in a single turn. The inclusion of powerful cards like Ancestral Recall, Time Walk, and Moxen help to further accelerate the deck's game plan and set up game-winning plays.

The deck is focused on controlling the opponent's strategy through counterspells and removal while utilizing card advantage through Lórien Revealed and other draw spells. It also looks to disrupt the opponent's mana base with Wasteland and Strip Mine. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion adds resilience and value to the deck. The aim is to outlast the opponent by efficiently answering threats and gaining incremental advantages until it can establish a dominant position and win the game.

The primary focus of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan by using hate cards like Sphere of Resistance, Null Rod, and Thorn of Amethyst while deploying aggressive creatures like Patchwork Automaton and Scrawling Crawler to pressure the opponent. The deck also aims to generate advantage and resilience through cards like Crucible of Worlds and Smokestack. Additionally, the deck utilizes powerful artifact ramp cards like Black Lotus and the Mox cycle to accelerate its game plan. With a mix of disruptive elements and aggressive threats, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly and efficiently.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game while utilizing the Oath of Druids strategy to cheat powerful creatures into play. The key strategy involves setting up Oath of Druids to quickly bring out Atraxa, Grand Unifier or other strong creatures like Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers. The deck also includes various control elements such as counterspells like Force of Will and disruption spells like Show and Tell to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Additionally, there are mana acceleration cards like Black Lotus and Moxen to help ramp up to play the powerful creatures.

The deck focuses on using cards like Thundertrap Trainer, Paradoxical Outcome, and Stock Up to generate card advantage and mana to set up powerful plays. It also includes cards like Underworld Breach and Flame of Anor to recur key pieces from the graveyard. The deck aims to assemble a combo win condition by generating a lot of mana and drawing through the deck quickly with spells like Time Walk, Brainstorm, and Demonic Tutor. The deck utilizes fast mana like the Moxen and powerful draw spells to set up explosive turns and win the game quickly.

The main focus of this deck is to maintain board control and disrupt the opponent's strategy while utilizing a combination of aggressive creatures and efficient spells. By playing disruptive creatures like Collector Ouphe and Deathrite Shaman, along with powerful counter spells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce, the deck aims to control the tempo of the game and apply pressure on the opponent. Additionally, the inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion provides additional value and recursion options for the deck's threats and disruption.

The deck focuses on using disruptive spells and creature removal in combination with efficient card draw to control the game pace. It includes a variety of counter spells to protect its game plan and cards like Demonic Tutor and Dig Through Time to find key answers or threats. Lurrus of the Dream-Den as the companion provides additional card advantage and recursion. The deck aims to out-value opponents while maintaining board control and disrupting their plans through a combination of removal, counter magic, and recursion.

This Doomsday combo deck aims to set up a winning game state by utilizing the card Doomsday to stack the top 5 cards of the library in a specific order that will lead to a guaranteed victory. The deck includes a variety of card draw, disruption, and fast mana to help find and play Doomsday as quickly as possible. Thassa's Oracle is used as the win condition once the Doomsday pile is set up, allowing the player to win the game on the spot. The deck focuses on assembling the combo efficiently while protecting it from opposing disruption.

This Vintage 1 vs 1 Doomsday Combo deck focuses on utilizing cards like Doomsday, Thassa's Oracle, and Psychic Frog to quickly assemble a winning combination. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect the combo. By combining card draw and control elements with powerful win conditions, this deck aims to smoothly navigate its way to victory against opponents. With the help of efficient mana acceleration from cards like Black Lotus and Moxen, the deck can quickly execute its game plan and secure a win.

The focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with efficient artifact creatures and disruption cards, while also using mana acceleration and lock pieces to control the board. It aims to put pressure on the opponent early on with aggressive creatures like Patchwork Automaton and Stonecoil Serpent, while also hindering their game plan with cards like Sphere of Resistance, Null Rod, and Trinisphere. The deck also includes cards like Golos, Tireless Pilgrim and Crucible of Worlds to ensure consistency and resilience. With a mix of aggression and disruption, this deck aims to quickly win the game before the opponent can stabilize.

This deck focuses on controlling the game with counterspells and disruption while utilizing the powerful Oath of Druids combo to cheat creatures into play, specifically Atraxa, Grand Unifier. The deck also includes other powerful cards like Force of Will, Show and Tell, and Oko, Thief of Crowns to maintain board control and pressure the opponent. The mana base is optimized to support the strategy, with fetch lands and dual lands to ensure consistency and the ability to cast spells on curve.

The deck focuses on quickly ramping into powerful threats like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and utilizing Oath of Druids to cheat out creatures. The control elements come in the form of counter spells like Force of Will and Force of Negation, as well as disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Mental Misstep. The deck also includes card advantage engines like Ancestral Recall and Stock Up to stay ahead in card advantage. Show and Tell can be used to quickly put out big threats like Bolas's Citadel, while Tinker can search for powerful artifacts like Black Lotus.

The deck focuses on efficient control and disruption, using powerful artifacts and countermagic to slow down the opponent's strategy and protect key combo pieces like Time Vault. It leverages powerful cards like Ancestral Recall and Force of Will to generate card advantage and stop threats, while also including several artifact tutors like Oswald Fiddlebender to find specific answers. With a strong mana base including powerful Moxen and utility lands like Tolarian Academy, the deck aims to control the game's tempo and win through incremental advantage and strategic plays.
